RULING: PLAINTIFF'S MOTION FOR ORDER GRANTING PREFERENCE IN SETTING CASE FOR TRIAL; AND EXTENDING DISCOVERY CUTOFF

Hearing held and tentative ruling adopted: Plaintiff's Motion for Order Granting Preference in Setting Case for Trial; and Extending Discovery Cutoff is GRANTED pursuant to C.C.P. 36(a). Defendant Fraser's Boiler Service Inc. contested the tentative ruling and informed the court of the end of their bankruptcy proceedings. No opposition filed. The trial date of December 9, 2019 is vacated. The new trial date is March 9, 2020 at 11:15 a.m. in Department 503. Last day pursuant to C.C.P. 36(f) is March 19, 2020. Local Rule 20 shall be followed by the parties as well as all San Francisco Local Rules and California Rules of Court. The last day for hearing summary judgment/adjudication motions is March 5, 2020. Summary judgment/adjudication motions shall be brought on regular notice pursuant to the relevant provisions of the C.C.P., unless the parties stipulate otherwise. The Court will allow a maximum of four summary judgment/adjudication motions per day to be calendared in this action. Before a party files and serves such motion, it must contact the Court's Calendar Clerk to make a reservation. Time to respond to written discovery that has not yet been served is shortened to 20 days. For written discovery that has already been served, responses are due within 20 days of this hearing or by the date determined by the C.C.P., whichever is earlier. If there is any kind of issue/dispute that requires meet and confer, such meet and confer shall not be made via email or letter. It shall occur in person or via telephone. Electronic service is considered the equivalent of personal service.
(Mini Minutes continued, see next entry)

Fact discovery cut-off date is February 21, 2020. Expert discovery cut-off date is March 6, 2020. All bankruptcy documents shall be turned over to Defendants no later than December 20, 2019. If Plaintiff submits documents to bankruptcy trusts after this date, he must notify Defendants of this no later than five days after submission. The fact discovery cut-off does not apply to bankruptcy documents.

No later than December 4, 2019, Plaintiff must provide a statement to each Defendant stating the name and contact information of each lay person with knowledge of any facts supporting any of Plaintiff's claims as to each Defendant. No later than December 4, 2019, Defendants must provide a statement stating the name and contact information of each lay person with knowledge of any facts supporting any of Defendant's affirmative defenses. Any person not identified on the statements may be barred from testifying at trial or presenting a declaration on a summary motion absent good cause shown. Further case management conference on January 22, 2020 at 1:30 p.m. No need for any party to file any case management conference statement. Parties may appear at the conference by telephone. If the parties agree that no case management conference is necessary, they shall notify the Court at least two day prior to take the CMC off calendar. Plaintiff to make a settlement demand on Defendants no later than January 31, 2020. Responses to the demand are due no later than February 14, 2020. This confidential value must be separately submitted to Commissioner Pang Ly via electronic mail by February 28, 2020 at 12:00 p.m. Mandatory settlement conference with only the attorneys present and final decision makers available by phone is set for Thursday, March 5, 2020 at 1:30 p.m. in 603. MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Nov-14-2019 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Andrew Chew of BRAYTON & PURCELL LLP (415-898-1555), appearing for plaintiff and opposing party Woody Sims.

RULING: DEFENDANT O'CONNOR LUMBER COMPANY'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Not argued and tentative ruling adopted: Defendant O'Connor Lumber Company's Motion for Summary Judgment is DENIED. Defendant failed to sustain its initial burden of demonstrating that Plaintiff Woody Sims does not possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence that he was exposed to asbestos-containing products or materials attributable to Defendant. (Aguilar v. Atlantic Richfield Co. (2001) 25 Cal.4th 826.) Defendant does not address Plaintiff's interrogatory response indicating that he purchased asbestos-containing plumbers putty at O'Connor Lumber Company in Vallejo, California. (Defendant's Ex. B at 3; Scheiding v. Dinwiddie Constr. Co. (1999) 69 Cal.App.4th 64, 81 [the court "can infer nothing at all with respect to questions which were neither asked nor answered"].) The Court does not address whether the exposure caused Plaintiff's injury, as argued in Defendant's moving papers, as Defendant did not address causation its separate statement of undisputed material facts. (United Cmty. Church v. Garcin (1991) 231 Cal. App. 3d 327, 337 (superseded by statute on other grounds as stated in Certain Underwriters at Lloyd's of London v. Superior Court (1997) 56 Cal.App.4th 952, 957, fn. 4).) Order signed in open court. Judge: Cynthia Ming-mei Lee, Clerk: A. Mok. Not reported. (503/CML)
